incorrects.description: Student selected 3 — counted only the full symbols, ignored the half-symbol

steps.text: You're working on it. Count the full symbols—that's 6. The half-symbol adds 1 more because half of 2 is 1.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6 — counted only the full symbols, missed that the half-symbol represents 1 more

steps.text: Let's think about this together. You counted the 3 full symbols—that's 6. But there's also a half-symbol. Half of 2 is 1, so add 1 more.

incorrects.description: Student selected 8 — likely treated the half-symbol as a full symbol or added incorrectly

steps.text: Here's a hint: each full symbol equals 2. Count the full symbols: 2, 4, 6. The half-symbol adds just 1 because half of 2 is 1.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: Animation replays: 3 full symbols light up sequentially (2, 4, 6), then half-symbol lights up showing +1 for total of 7

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Each full symbol equals 2 sightings. So 3 full symbols: that's 2, 4, 6. The half-symbol adds 1 more because half of 2 is 1. 6 plus 1 equals 7 sightings. There we go.
